content: The review-staging step must apply proposals in chronological order so that later proposals (e.g., a rejection at seq=17) override earlier proposals (e.g., a decision at seq=5) rather than both being applied blindly. Raw proposals correctly record what was said at the time — they should not be retroactively edited. The ordering is what ensures consistency.

provenance.raw_agent: seq5-1 still has CodeHealth in it — the agent correctly recorded what was said at the time. The rejection in seq17 is a separate proposal. The review-staging step needs to be smart about applying proposals in order, so seq17 overrides seq5-1 when writing to the wiki rather than both being applied blindly.
provenance.raw_user: It's kind of useful to be able to look at the output though. We can always roll back the commit
